data.title: Equinox Gold hits three-year high as first ore processed at Valentine mine
data.description:
Equinox Gold (NYSE:EQX) edges up in Thursday's trading to its highest in more than three years after saying its Valentine gold mine in Newfoundland & Labrador has begun processing ore through its 2.5M metric tons/year facility, and expecting to pour first gold within the next month.

Equinox (NYSE:EQX) is well positioned to ramp up to nameplate capacity at the Valentine mine during Q2 2026, when it expects to produce 175K-200K oz/year of gold for the first 12 years of its 14-year reserve life.

Once fully operational, Valentine will be Equinox Gold's (EQX) second largest mine and the largest gold mine in Atlantic Canada; the company obtained the mine through its recent acquisition of Calibre Mining.

Valentine is the second mine Equinox (EQX) has brought online, following the start-up of its Greenstone project in Ontario, which entered commercial production in November 2024.

Equinox (EQX) is "entering a pivotal phase of growth, with production and cash flow expected to increase meaningfully as Valentine ramps up and Greenstone approaches nameplate capacity," CEO Darren Hall said.

data.title: CrowdStrike's ARR growth provides sign of durability heading into next year: analysts
data.description:
CrowdStrike's (NASDAQ:CRWD) second quarter results demonstrated higher than expected net new annual recurring revenue, prompting mostly positive reviews from financial firms.

Shares edged up 2% during Thursday trading.

"The most incremental point in our view is that 2H25 NNARR is expected to grow at least 40% which results in a 22%+ exit rate on ARR as some portion of CCP’s will come back – the debate however will continue on whether this is a 20% grower next year," said Barclays analysts Saket Kalia and Alyssa Lee, in an investor note.

Barclays maintained its Overweight rating and $500 price target on the cybersecurity stock.

"The main nitpick in our view is that FY26 revenue guide is only nudging up at the bottom end to $4,749-$4,805, from $4,743-$4,805 previously, which compounds the fact that FY26 revenue guide did not go up in 1Q," Kalia added.

Similarly, RBC Capital Markets maintained its Outperform rating and $510 price target.

"The combination of early NNARR acceleration and Re-Flex success gives us confidence in back-half acceleration and momentum continuing into FY/27," said RBC analysts, led by Matthew Hedberg, in a note. "That said, there will continue to be questions around the divergence between ARR and subscription revenue as CCP impacts contra revenue."

However, Goldman Sachs lowered its price target to $492 from $530.

"3Q revenue was 1% below the Street, with CrowdStrike noting that its CCP partner rebate program gets amortized as contra revenue," said Goldman Sachs analysts Gabriela Borges and Max Gamperl, in a note. "We believe the key to the stock beginning to outperform again will be the company returning to a more consistent beat and raise across NNARR, revenue and EPS; which we expect will be in 1HFY26 when abnormal revenue recognition tied to concessions abate."

Other cybersecurity stocks were mostly up as well on Thursday. Palo Alto Networks (PANW) inched up 1%, Zscaler (ZS) had climbed 3% and Fortinet (FTNT) had increased 1.3%.

data.title: Market optimism continues as the S&P 500 tallies 19 record highs in 2025
data.description:
Wall Street’s benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) extended its record-setting momentum on Wednesday, closing at another all-time high—the 19th such milestone of 2025. The index’s steady climb underscores the market’s resilience, though it still trails last year’s remarkable run, when the S&P 500 registered 57 record highs in 2024.

As of Thursday, the index is trading at 6,483, hovering near the flatline as investors weigh fresh economic data and corporate earnings against broader market optimism. 

For a clearer perspective on the index’s performance, Carson Investment Research has provided the following visual chart.

Furthermore, see what some Seeking Alpha analysts have to say about the overall state of the S&P 500 and U.S. economy:

SA analyst Bret Jensen said: “U.S. equities are historically overvalued by multiple historical metrics, with profit margins and valuation ratios at unsustainable highs amid weak economic growth,” in Say It With Me. We Are In An 'Everything' Bubble.

SA analyst Multiplo Invest stated: “I reiterate my buy recommendation on assets that track the main American indices… Investors are still extremely confident in US assets, especially with the increased likelihood of interest cuts as early as September,” in Since 1987, This Same Uncertainty Has Been A Buying Opportunity. 

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Wall Street looks for direction after a slew of economic data and NVDA earnings results
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes are searching for a clear direction on Thursday, as investors weigh a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, a day after the S&P 500 secured a record close.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) finds itself down by 0.2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) hovers near the flatline,  and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) moved higher by 0.3%.

From a sector-by-sector vantage point, four of the 11 S&P segments are up in the green with Communication Services and Info Tech leading the way. On the other end, Health Care has suffered the most so far on the day. 

Shifting to the bond market, the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) has moved down 2 basis points to 4.21%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) has moved higher by 2 basis points to 3.63%.

On the economic front, Q2 GDP growth was revised higher to 3.3%, while the PCE increase was revised lower to 2.0%. 

Furthermore, initial jobless claims  unexpectedly fell in the past week. Q2 corporate profits bounced back, rising 2.0%. 

Additionally, pending home sales dipped 0.4% in July, which was weaker than economists had forecasted. 

Lisa Cook filed a lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump's move to oust her from the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, escalating the conflict between the White House and the U.S. central bank.

Nvidia (NVDA) remains in focus as shares dip 1% after the firm reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations and unveiled a massive share buyback program.

As for stocks that are on move, shares of Snowflake (SNOW) have surged 18.5% after the company produced a strong earnings report.

data.title: Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Composite holds steady in August
data.description:
Kansas City Manufacturing Composite Index held at 1 in August, unchanged from July, the district Federal Reserve Bank said on Thursday.

"The durable manufacturing sector was mostly flat, while nondurable manufacturing activity declined slightly driven by printing activities and paper and chemical manufacturing," the Kansas City Fed said.

Production edged up to 0 from -3, while the employment index improve to 0 from -11.

Most month-over-month indexes were positive, except for the backlog of orders, new export orders, and inventories, the report said.

data.title: Market sentiment remains bullish, shows AAII data
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors rose for the second consecutive week, showed the recent survey conducted by American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

The American Association of Individual Investors said in its survey that the bullish camp rose to 34.6% for the week ended August 27 from 30.8% the week before. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment as per the survey fell to 39.4% for the week, versus 44.8% the week before.

Even though investors braced themselves for more uncertainties amid the U.S. - China trade conflict and weak quarterly reports from some major retail and tech companies, rising prospects of Federal Reserve lowering interest rates for the first time this year in September remains a welcoming theme for the market.

The benchmark S&P500 (SP500) and Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gained around 1.3% and 2% in the last one week, while Dow (DJI) rose around 1.6%.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also rose during the week, with the figure standing at 26.0%, compared to 24.4% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

 

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Boeing seeks EU clearance for $4.7B Spirit AeroSystems deal
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) formally requested approval from European Union regulators to reacquire Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR), according to filings published on the European Commission’s website. Spirit, the world’s largest independent manufacturer of aircraft structures, was originally spun off from Boeing (NYSE:BA) in 2005.

The U.S. planemaker announced in July 2024 that it had struck a $4.7 billion all-stock agreement to bring Spirit (NYSE:SPR) back under its ownership. As part of the arrangement, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) would absorb Spirit’s (SPR) operations tied to its European programs, which have been running at a loss.

The European Commission is scheduled to deliver its ruling on the proposed transaction by September 30. Earlier this month, the UK Competition and Markets Authority cleared the deal, concluding that it did not warrant a deeper probe into potential antitrust concerns.

data.title: Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway boosts stakes in Japanese trading houses
data.description:
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) (NYSE:BRK.A) increased his stakes in Japanese trading houses Mitsubishi Corp. (OTCPK:MSBHF) and Mitsui Corp. (OTCPK:MITSY) (OTCPK:MITSF), the Japanese companies said, according to a media report.

The insurance and investing firm boosted its stake in Mitsubishi to 10.23% from 9.74% as of Aug. 28, 2025, Mitsubishi said in a filing.

Separately, Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) also increased its holdings in Mitsui (OTCPK:MITSY), but didn't disclose the amount, as its holdings remain below 10% of the company's outstanding stock, Bloomberg reported, citing a statement from a Mitsui spokesperson. The Omaha, Nebraska-based company held 9.82% of Mitsui as of March, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) also has holdings in Marubeni Corp. (OTCPK:MARUY) (OTCPK:MARUF), Itochu Corp. (OTCPK:ITOCF) (OTCPK:ITOCY), and Sumitomo Corp.(OTCPK:SSUMY) (OTCPK:SSUMF).

The U.S. conglomerate started buying shares in the sprawling Japanese companies about six years ago Initially, Berkshire (BRK.B) agreed to keep its holdings of each of the five Japanese companies below 10%, but "as we approached this limit, the five companies agreed to moderately relax the ceiling," Buffett said in his annual letter to Berkshire stockholders in February. "Over time, you will likely see Berkshire's ownership of all five increase somewhat."

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch teams with Dallas Cowboys in latest NFL fashion play
data.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) has been named the official fashion partner of the Dallas Cowboys and Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders just days after the retailer was  named the official fashion partner for the National Football League.

Men’s and women’s apparel, including outerwear and accessories, representing teams of the NFL are now available at select Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) stores and online.

As an official fashion partner of the Dallas Cowboys, Abercrombie will offer its licensed Cowboys collection for sale at select ANF stores and online, as well at select Cowboys pro-shops and a prominent presence at both AT&T Stadium and The Star in Frisco, the Cowboys' headquarters and practice facility

Additionally, the retail brand will have a presence at team events including home games, the Cowboys Club and Dallas Cowboys Women’s Association events.

The company is also teaming up with Cowboys wide receiver CeeDee Lamb and tight end Jack Ferguson in its “Style Concierge” program to provide select NFL players access to Abercrombie (NYSE:ANF) stylists for “off-the-field” looks.

“The NFL and fashion are more aligned than ever, which makes this partnership with Abercrombie an impactful step forward,” said Dallas Cowboys chief brand officer and co-owner Charlotte Jones in a statement.

Today’s announcement regarding the Cowboys and ANF along with the recent tie-up between American Eagle (AEO) and Kansas City tight end Travis Kelce, underscores the NFL’s growing pull with retailers seeking cultural relevance.

data.title: Silgan, International Paper upgraded at Bank of America on valuation, pricing
data.description:
Bank of America Securities on Thursday raised its ratings on Silgan Holdings (NYSE:SLGN) and International Paper (NYSE:IP) to Buy from Neutral, citing attractive valuations and stronger-than-expected containerboard pricing that could offset operational headwinds.

The call follows a volatile second-quarter earnings season, in which packaging and paperboard stocks fell about 5% since June 30, underperforming the Standard & Poor’s 500 stock index (SP500). Analyst George Staphos said recent price action has created opportunities in the sector as investors cautiously shift back to a more “risk-on” posture.

Silgan: Pullback creates entry point

Silgan (NYSE:SLGN) shares dropped more than 15% after the company lowered earnings guidance, citing weak beverage closure volumes and a food-can customer bankruptcy. Staphos argued the sell-off was overdone, given the company now trades at roughly 11 to 12 times forward earnings, below peers that average closer to 14 times.

While defensive elements remain because of food cans, the analysts said they believe the downside is priced in and they see room for volumes to improve. Bank of America also highlighted upside for other packaging names, including Sealed Air (SEE), Sonoco Products (SON) and Graphic Packaging Holding (GPK).

International Paper: Pricing offsets execution risk

For International Paper (NYSE:IP), Bank of America acknowledged ongoing execution risk following its pulp divestiture and restructuring efforts. Still, the firm sees pricing power as a decisive factor. The bank lifted its 2026 forecasts, adding another $40 per ton increase in the third quarter of 2026 on top of an earlier $40 per ton bump for the first quarter. Its projected $60 per ton rise is well above consensus estimates of $20 to $30, according to Visible Alpha data cited by the bank.

Operating rates should exceed 95% by 2026, and if the bank’s pricing assumptions hold, that will more than offset near-term operational challenges, Staphos said. Packaging Corp. of America (PKG), already rated Buy, stands to benefit even more directly given its cleaner profile without restructuring complications.

Sector still acts like ‘bonds in disguise’

Despite pricing momentum, Bank of America cautioned that aggregate volume growth in packaging remains sluggish, averaging just 1% to 3% since the sector’s recession ended in late 2023. Staphos reiterated his longstanding view that packaging stocks often behave “like bonds masquerading as equities,” delivering strong cash flows but limited growth. Value creation, he added, depends on disciplined capital allocation and shareholder returns, rather than large-scale acquisitions unless tied to commercial gains.

With the upgrades, Bank of America now holds Buy ratings across all three containerboard producers it covers: International Paper (IP), Packaging Corp. of America (PKG) and WestRock rival Graphic Packaging (GPK). The ratings reflect a more optimistic stance on sector fundamentals heading into 2026.
